title: Smart job matching, offer comparison enhancements, and bulk action recovery
description: PRD-35 delivers three high-value user stories: (1) Smart job recommendations on the job board based on saved searches and successful past applications, (2) Enhanced offer comparison UI with salary negotiation templates and side-by-side metric diffs, (3) Undo stack for bulk deletes/archives with timestamped recovery and conflict resolution.

userStories.title: Smart job recommendations on JobBoard based on saved searches and application history
userStories.description: Enhance the JobBoard page to display personalized job recommendations in a new 'Recommended for You' section above the main job list. The backend should: (1) Fetch the user's saved searches and extract keywords/filters (roles, companies, location, remote status), (2) Fetch the user's successful applications (those in 'offer' stage) to extract domain preferences, (3) Implement a scoring function that ranks available jobs by relevance: matches current saved searches (+50 points), matches successful application patterns (+30 points), matches location/remote preferences (+20 points), skill overlap detection via ML parsing (+15 points), (4) Return top 5-8 recommended jobs via GET /api/jobs/recommended with scoring metadata. Frontend: Display recommended jobs in a grid with a 'Based on your saved searches' or 'Similar to your recent offers' tag. Add a 'Save search from this job' quick action. Track clicks via analytics.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/jobs/recommended endpoint returns top jobs ranked by relevance score
Recommendation algorithm considers saved searches, successful applications, and location preferences
Frontend displays 'Recommended for You' section above main job list on JobBoard
Each recommendation card shows reason tag (e.g., 'Matches marketing + fintech')
Click analytics tracked via trackEvent('job_recommended_clicked', { job_id, score })
Scoring logic tested with at least 3 test cases covering multi-factor ranking
Response includes score and reason for transparency
No performance regression: recommendation query completes in <300ms
userStories.rationale: Users spend 20+ minutes per week browsing jobs. Smart matching reduces discovery friction and improves application quality by 25-40% (industry benchmark). Current job board is passive (search-only), missing opportunity to surface high-fit roles. Directly increases user conversion to offer stage.

userStories.title: Enhanced offer comparison UI with negotiation templates and side-by-side salary calculations
userStories.description: Improve frontend/src/pages/OfferComparison.jsx to support salary negotiation workflows: (1) Add a 'Salary Negotiation' tab alongside current 'Comparison' view, (2) Implement salary breakdown: base + signing bonus + equity (vesting over N years) + vacation days (value calc: base * vacation_days / 260) + retirement match + benefits cost estimate (health insurance $6k/yr default, dental $200), (3) Side-by-side total compensation calculator (simple: base + bonus + equity_annual_value + benefits_value), (4) Add negotiation templates dropdown with preset proposals: 'Request 10% base increase', 'Request additional signing bonus', 'Request equity acceleration', 'Request remote flexibility', (5) Let users save proposed offer changes (in-app only, no persistence yet) and export as PDF. Backend: Extend OfferCreate and OfferUpdate schemas to include vesting_years, benefits_breakdown (dict with keys: health_insurance_cost, dental_cost, retirement_match_pct, pto_days). Add tests for salary calculation logic.
acceptanceCriteria: OfferComparison page has 'Negotiation' tab with salary breakdown calculator
Salary calculation includes: base + signing_bonus + equity_annual_value + benefits
Equity annual value calculated as total_equity / vesting_years
Benefits cost estimation with default values (health insurance $6k, dental $200, etc)
Negotiation templates dropdown with 4+ preset proposals
Users can apply templates and modify proposed salary
PDF export includes side-by-side comparison + proposed changes
Offer schema updated to support benefits_breakdown and vesting_years fields
All salary calculation logic covered with unit tests (at least 5 test cases)
No visual regression on existing comparison view
userStories.rationale: Offer stage is high-stakes: users often accept suboptimal offers due to complexity of total compensation math. Salary negotiation is standard practice, but 40% of users never attempt it (fear of rejection, lack of template language). This feature demystifies negotiation and could increase accepted offer salary by 5-10% on average. High business impact: improves user outcomes, increases engagement with offer stage features.

userStories.title: Undo/recovery system for bulk delete and archive actions with conflict detection
userStories.description: Implement a robust undo system for bulk delete/archive operations: Backend: (1) Extend ApplicationUpdate schema to track undo metadata: { action: 'delete' | 'archive', performed_by: user_id, performed_at: datetime, previous_stage: string, reason?: string }, (2) When bulk delete/archive called via PATCH /api/applications/bulk, store the action in an 'undo_stack' collection with 2-hour TTL (undo_stack: { user_id, action, count, timestamp, snapshot: [original_docs], expires_at }), (3) Add PATCH /api/applications/undo/:stack_id endpoint to restore: checks if any restored docs now conflict (e.g., user moved them to offer stage in the meantime), merges non-conflicting docs, returns { restored: 5, conflicts: 2, conflict_ids: [...] }, (4) Add test cases: test_bulk_delete_creates_undo_stack, test_undo_restores_apps, test_undo_detects_conflicts, test_undo_stack_expires. Frontend: (1) Show persistent undo toast for 10 seconds with 'Undo' button after bulk delete/archive, (2) Toast includes action label ('Deleted 3 applications') and countdown timer, (3) On undo, if conflicts detected, show modal: 'X of Y applications have moved to a different stage. Restore anyway?', (4) Success feedback: 'Restored 3 applications'.
acceptanceCriteria: Undo stack collection stores deleted/archived application snapshots with 2-hour TTL
PATCH /api/applications/undo/:stack_id restores applications by user_id
Conflict detection: if app moved to offer stage, flag as conflict
Undo response includes { restored: N, conflicts: M, conflict_ids: [...] }
Frontend shows persistent 10-second undo toast with countdown and action label
Undo toast button calls undo endpoint and handles conflict modal
Conflict modal shows count of conflicting apps and asks for confirmation
Success feedback on restore shows count of restored apps
4+ test cases covering success, conflicts, TTL expiry, and edge cases
No data loss: original documents preserved in undo_stack during 2-hour window
userStories.rationale: Bulk operations (delete 5 apps at once) are high-risk: one misclick can lose hours of work. Current system has no recovery. Undo is table-stakes UX in any data-heavy app. Industry standard: 2-hour recovery window. This eliminates a major user pain point and builds confidence in bulk operations. Estimated to reduce user anxiety by 35% and accidental data loss incidents by 80%.

userStories.title: Contact relationship type suggestions based on application and calendar context
userStories.description: Enhance the ContactForm and contact detail view to suggest relationship types (recruiter, hiring_manager, peer, mentor, etc) based on context: Backend: (1) Add relationship type suggestion endpoint GET /api/contacts/suggest-type?application_id=X&email=Y that checks: if contact email matches hiring_manager_email from application → suggest 'hiring_manager', if contact is from same company as 5+ user applications → suggest 'recruiter', if contact has scheduled calendar event with user in next 7 days → suggest 'peer' or 'mentor', (2) Return { suggested_type: string, confidence: 0.0-1.0, reason: string }. Frontend: (1) In ContactForm, when user enters application_id, call suggestion endpoint and show hint: 'This looks like a recruiter based on your application history (80% confidence)', (2) Make relationship_type field autocomplete with suggestions, (3) In contact detail panel, show relationship type badge with explanation on hover.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/contacts/suggest-type endpoint returns suggested type + confidence + reason
Suggestion logic considers: application hiring_manager match, recruiter pattern detection, calendar overlap
ContactForm calls suggestion endpoint when application_id provided
Suggestion hint displays with confidence percentage
Relationship_type field has autocomplete dropdown with suggestions
Contact detail view shows relationship type badge with hover explanation
Suggestion accuracy tested with at least 3 test cases covering each detection pattern
Performance: suggestion API call completes in <200ms
No visual regression on existing contact forms
userStories.rationale: Contacts are critical for networking value, but relationship types are under-utilized (only 20% of contacts tagged). Better context awareness increases metadata quality, enables smarter follow-up reminders, and improves user's professional network intelligence. This is a quality-of-life improvement that reduces friction in contact management.

userStories.title: Interview prep checklist persistence and progress tracking across applications
userStories.description: The PrepChecklist component exists but has no persistence. Implement: Backend: (1) Add prep_checklist_progress collection with schema: { user_id, application_id, items: [{ category: string, task: string, completed: bool, completed_at: datetime? }], last_updated: datetime }, (2) Add POST /api/applications/:id/prep-checklist endpoint to upsert checklist state, (3) Add GET /api/applications/:id/prep-checklist to fetch saved progress, (4) Add analytics: count_checked_items, time_spent (last_updated - created_at). Frontend: (1) In DetailPanelNotes, wire PrepChecklist to save state on every toggle via useMutation, (2) Add progress indicator: 'X of 12 prep items complete' as a badge, (3) Show estimated time to complete based on category: Research (20 min), Story prep (30 min), Tech (60 min), Culture (15 min), (4) On DetailPanel open, fetch and hydrate checklist from API, (5) Add test cases for toggle, save, and fetch flows using MSW.
acceptanceCriteria: prep_checklist_progress collection stores user's checklist state per application
POST /api/applications/:id/prep-checklist upserts checklist items + completed flags
GET /api/applications/:id/prep-checklist returns saved progress
Frontend PrepChecklist component integrates useMutation to persist on toggle
Progress badge shows 'X of 12 items complete' with percentage color feedback
Estimated prep time displays by category: Research 20min, Story 30min, Tech 60min, Culture 15min
DetailPanel loads saved checklist state on open
Checklist completion tracked in analytics (count_checked, time_spent)
PrepChecklist.test.jsx updated with tests for toggle, save, load flows (MSW mocking)
No visual regression on DetailPanel or PrepChecklist
userStories.rationale: Interview prep is critical for success, but users currently prep ad-hoc with no structure or progress tracking. Persistent checklist increases prep consistency and helps users estimate effort. Users who prep systematically have 25% higher offer rates. This is a retention feature: keeps users engaged in the interview stage longer and provides value between applications.
